evals.prompt: Hey, I need to scrape the Wikipedia page for 'Web scraping' (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_scraping) and extract all the section headings (h2 and h3) along with the text content under each section. Save the result as a structured JSON file. The page is static HTML so you shouldn't need a browser — just use httpx and BeautifulSoup.
evals.expected_output: A JSON file with the page's section structure: headings and their associated text content
expectations: Used httpx or requests (not Playwright) for HTTP request
Output is a valid JSON file
JSON contains at least 5 section entries
Each entry has a 'heading' field and a 'content' field
The heading 'Techniques' or similar major section is present in the output

evals.prompt: I need to scrape product listings from https://books.toscrape.com/ — it's a fake bookstore site made for practicing scraping. I need: the title, price, and availability status of every book on the first page. Save the results as a CSV file. The site is static so just use httpx and BeautifulSoup.
evals.expected_output: A CSV file with columns: title, price, availability — containing all ~20 books from the first page
expectations: Used httpx or requests (not Playwright) for HTTP request
Output is a valid CSV file
CSV has at least 15 rows (most of the 20 books on the page)
CSV has columns: title, price, and availability (or in_stock / status)
At least one book's price is extracted correctly (e.g., '£' or number format)

evals.prompt: I need to scrape https://quotes.toscrape.com/js/ — this site loads quotes via JavaScript. I need to extract all quotes from all pages (there are 10 pages, each with 10 quotes). For each quote I need: the text, the author, and the tags. Save to a JSON file. Since the content is JS-rendered, you'll need Playwright. Handle the pagination too.
evals.expected_output: A JSON file with 100 quotes (10 pages × 10 quotes each), each with text, author, and tags
expectations: Used Playwright (not just httpx/requests) since content is JS-rendered
Output is a valid JSON file
JSON contains at least 90 quotes (near 100 total across all pages)
Each quote has 'text', 'author', and 'tags' fields
Author names are present (e.g., 'Marilyn Monroe', 'Albert Einstein')
Quotes include content beyond just the first page (multiple pages scraped)

evals.prompt: I need to scrape data from a Cloudflare-protected website at https://nowsecure.nl/ (a known CF-protected test page). Write a Python script that uses curl_cffi with browser impersonation to bypass the Cloudflare challenge and extract the page title and any visible text content. Save the results as a JSON file. Include retry logic that refreshes the approach if the first attempt gets blocked (403/503). Also include a FlareSolverr fallback function (even if FlareSolverr is not running, the function should be defined and documented).
evals.expected_output: A Python script using curl_cffi with impersonate='chrome', retry logic for 403/503, a FlareSolverr fallback function, and a JSON output file with extracted content
expectations: Script uses curl_cffi (not requests/httpx) as the primary HTTP client
curl_cffi requests use impersonate parameter (e.g., 'chrome')
Script includes retry logic for 403/503 status codes
Script defines a FlareSolverr fallback function for solving CF challenges
Output is a valid JSON file with extracted page content
